title: The Sylvester Stallone Hall of Fame With Bill Simmons
format: Sean Fennessey and Bill Simmons build a Sylvester Stallone Hall of Fame, sorting fifty-plus movies into green, yellow, and red before landing on a final ten.

segments.heading: Sly Stallone, in Broad Strokes
segments.note: the eras, the zags, and the bets he placed on himself

films.title: Samaritan
films.note: The new one, on Amazon Prime and the excuse for the episode. Both agree it's bad; Simmons compares it to Judge Dredd.

films.title: Judge Dredd
films.note: His earlier run at a superhero movie. "Judge Dredd was bad and it is not in my hall of fame."

films.title: Driven
films.note: A cool-crime-movie zag with Burt Reynolds and Kip Pardue that just failed. Simmons: they looked like Halloween masks of their own faces.

films.title: Get Carter
films.note: Another attempted zag into a prestige crime picture. Didn't work.

films.title: Cop Land
films.note: The zag that did work: an overweight, accented, ground-level New Jersey cop, playing against Robert De Niro.

films.title: Cliffhanger
films.note: Simmons's pick for the best movie Stallone made in the '90s; a hit, though not Rambo-sized.

films.title: Rocky
films.note: The original bet on himself: he wrote it and refused to hand the part to anyone else.

films.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II
films.note: 1985 is his apex: this and Rocky IV each made $300 million internationally, the biggest money in movies at the time.

films.title: Rocky IV
films.note: The other half of his $600 million worldwide year.

films.title: Staying Alive
films.note: Simmons was a "season ticket guy" for Stallone through Cop Land; he saw this one in theaters because Stallone directed it.

films.title: Tango & Cash
films.note: Referenced as a recent Rewatchables pick, coincidentally airing the same week as this episode.

films.title: The Expendables
films.note: The franchise that revived him in Hollywood, whether or not anyone loves the actual movies.

films.title: Rocky Balboa
films.note: The comeback picture. Simmons admits he was too hard on it at the time; audiences were just happy to have the character back after Rocky V.

films.title: Rambo
films.note: Part of a run of three straight comeback hits: Rocky Balboa, this, and The Expendables.

films.title: Creed III
films.note: Due out next year; word at the time of the episode is Stallone isn't in it, which they tie to his public fight over Rocky's ownership.

segments.heading: The Ballot
segments.note: green, yellow, or red, walked chronologically through the filmography

films.title: The Party at Kitty and Stud's
films.note: His soft-core start, later reissued as The Italian Stallion once Rocky made him famous. "This is obviously not in the Hall of Fame."

films.title: MASH
films.note: One of several early films where he's basically an extra.

films.title: Bananas
films.note: A Woody Allen film; another glorified-extra credit.

films.title: Klute
films.note: Same era, same kind of stand-in role.

films.title: The Prisoner of Second Avenue
films.note: Another bit part before he was anybody.

films.title: No Place to Hide
films.note: Neither of them has seen it. "There's no way that's going."

films.title: The Lords of Flatbush
films.note: His first real starring-ish role, a Happy Days-adjacent greaser movie. Not going in, though it's important to his career.

films.title: Capone
films.note: He plays Frank Nitti opposite Ben Gazzara. "Okay movie, not a Hall of Famer."

films.title: Death Race 2000
films.note: A Roger Corman Rollerball ripoff with Stallone in a supporting role. Not in.

films.title: F.I.S.T.
films.note: A Jimmy Hoffa-style union-leader drama, rewritten by Stallone from Joe Eszterhas's original draft. Debated as an easy yellow but ultimately: "Fist can't make it."

films.title: Paradise Alley
films.note: He wrote and directed this one too, playing Cosmo Carboni. "No good."

films.title: Rocky II
films.note: In, mainly for the last 25 minutes, which Fennessey calls arguably the best stretch of the whole franchise.

films.title: Nighthawks
films.note: A cop-hunts-a-terrorist thriller with Rutger Hauer's U.S. debut. Liked, not inducted.

films.title: Escape to Victory
films.note: Also known just as Victory, with Pelé and Michael Caine. A John Huston-directed soccer movie with a legendary sequence and a slow build. Left as a yellow.

films.title: Rocky III
films.note: Automatic green; called one of the most satisfying, no-minute-wasted movies he ever made.

films.title: First Blood
films.note: Automatic green. Called the best-acted performance of his career, and a movie that holds up far better than its sequels suggest.

films.title: Rhinestone
films.note: The Dolly Parton musical zag. Simmons's hot take: it's actually not bad, unlike Staying Alive. Not inducted.

films.title: Cobra
films.note: A Cannon Films production with a death-cult villain and a chopped-down, incoherent cut. Debated hard against Over the Top for the last spot and wins it, since the Hall of Fame needs at least one full-Cannon, flying-too-close-to-the-sun Stallone entry.

films.title: Over the Top
films.note: The arm-wrestling family movie, a sentimental favorite for Simmons. Loses the final debate to Cobra.

films.title: Rambo III
films.note: A disappointment, arriving with no real geopolitical enemy left to fight. Not in.

films.title: Lock Up
films.note: A prison movie with a famous football sequence. A hard yellow, but not enough cachet to make the cut.

films.title: Tango & Cash
films.note: Yellow, discussed for the higher tier, but ultimately: "Is Tango & Cash better than Rocky II? No."

films.title: Rocky V
films.note: Hard red. "Blow it up with a bunch of dog shit and send it to the sun."

films.title: Oscar
films.note: A John Landis screwball comedy that Simmons calls one of the worst movies any famous star ever made. Not in.

films.title: Stop! Or My Mom Will Shoot
films.note: Grouped with Rocky V and Oscar as the career-low stretch. Not in.

films.title: Demolition Man
films.note: A sci-fi satire with Wesley Snipes and an early Sandra Bullock role, and arguably his funniest performance. A hard yellow, but not one of the final ten.

films.title: The Specialist
films.note: A Sharon Stone vehicle remembered mostly for an infamous, oddly shot sex scene. Not in.

films.title: Assassins
films.note: With Antonio Banderas at his post-Desperado peak. A good idea for a movie that never comes together. Not in.

films.title: Daylight
films.note: A Speed-in-a-tunnel disaster movie. Left in as a yellow "out of respect," but not going in.

films.title: Cop Land
films.note: Called an all-time scorching yellow that ultimately makes the cut, for showing he can hold his own in a dramatic ensemble with De Niro and Harvey Keitel.

films.title: D-Tox
films.note: Also released as Eye See You, part of a three-year, five-movie dead stretch with Charles S. Dutton and Kris Kristofferson. Not discussed as a candidate.

films.title: Avenging Angelo
films.note: Same dry stretch. Not discussed as a candidate.

films.title: Shade
films.note: Same dry stretch. Not discussed as a candidate.

films.title: Spy Kids 3-D: Game Over
films.note: His villain role that briefly put him back in the mainstream during the nine-year gap after Cop Land. Not a candidate.

films.title: Bullet to the Head
films.note: A Walter Hill picture that Fennessey remembers as "pretty solid" but definitely not a Hall of Famer.

films.title: Creed
films.note: Called an easy, no-debate in, even with five Rocky-adjacent films already on the list. "It just has to be in."

segments.heading: The Sylvester Stallone Hall of Fame
segments.note: the final ten, in release order

films.title: Rocky
films.note: The Best Picture winner and the character's origin. "There is no Rocky without Sylvester Stallone."

films.title: Rocky II
films.note: In, largely for its final 25 minutes.

films.title: Rocky III
films.note: "Rocky III is incredible. There's not a minute wasted."

films.title: First Blood
films.note: The best-acted performance of his career, and closer to a survival thriller than the sequels suggest.

films.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II
films.note: One half of his 1985 apex, alongside Rocky IV.

films.title: Rocky IV
films.note: The other half; "it ended the Cold War," with Dolph Lundgren's Drago as one of the great villains.

films.title: Cobra
films.note: Wins the debate over Over the Top for the last, flying-too-close-to-the-sun slot.

films.title: Cliffhanger
films.note: "There's no question it's in. There's zero debate."

films.title: Cop Land
films.note: His case for range: a low-key, dramatic performance across from De Niro and Keitel.

films.title: Creed
films.note: The reinvention. "It just has to be in."

films: The Best Little Whorehouse in Texas

The Party at Kitty and Stud's.title: The Party at Kitty and Stud's

Stop! Or My Mom Will Shoot.title: Stop! Or My Mom Will Shoot

The Best Little Whorehouse in Texas.title: The Best Little Whorehouse in Texas
